files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+{
+  "permissions": {
+    "allow": [
+      "WebSearch",
+      "WebFetch(domain:stackoverflow.com)",
+      "WebFetch(domain:medium.com)",
+      "WebFetch(domain:readmedium.com)",
+      "WebFetch(domain:spring.io)",
+      "WebFetch(domain:alexanderobregon.substack.com)",
+      "WebFetch(domain:bugs.openjdk.org)",
+      "Bash(git log:*)",
+      "WebFetch(domain:wiki.openjdk.org)",
+      "Bash(curl:*)",
+      "Bash(mvn clean compile:*)",
+      "Bash(./build.sh compile:*)",
+      "Bash(java:*)",
+      "Bash(mvn -pl core/org.openjdk.jmc.common clean compile:*)",
+      "Bash(mvn spotless:apply:*)",
+      "Bash(./build.sh:*)",
+      "Bash(ls:*)",
+      "Bash(git add:*)",
+      "Bash(git commit:*)",
+      "Bash(mvn:*)",
+      "Bash(javap:*)",
+      "Bash(javac:*)",
+      "Bash(git ls-tree:*)",
+      "Bash(while read hash msg)",
+      "Bash(do if git show $hash:.gitignore)",
+      "Bash(then echo \"$hash $msg\")",
+      "Bash(break)",
+      "Bash(fi)",
+      "Bash(done)",
+      "Bash(find:*)",
+      "Bash(git checkout:*)",
+      "Bash(git reset:*)"
+    ],
+    "deny": [],
+    "ask": []
+  },
+  "enableAllProjectMcpServers": true,
+  "enabledMcpjsonServers": [
+    "supabase"
+  ]
+}

+	/**
+	 * Checks if an attribute's ContentType is a filterable numeric type even without a Persister.
+	 * These types (COUNT, INDEX, RAW_NUMBER, RAW_LONG, etc.) can be compared using
+	 * ItemFilters.equals() and should appear in the "Add Filter from Attribute" menu.
+	 *
+	 * @param attribute
+	 *            the attribute to check
+	 * @return true if the attribute has a filterable numeric ContentType
+	 */
+	private static boolean isFilterableNumericType(IAttribute<?> attribute) {
+		org.openjdk.jmc.common.unit.ContentType<?> ct = attribute.getContentType();
+		// Check for known numeric types that don't have a Persister but support filtering
+		// RAW_NUMBER: used by primitive types (byte, short, int, long, float, double, char)
+		// RAW_LONG: used by some custom Long attributes
+		// COUNT, INDEX, IDENTIFIER: legacy types, rarely used but included for completeness
+		return ct.equals(UnitLookup.RAW_NUMBER) || ct.equals(UnitLookup.RAW_LONG) || ct.equals(UnitLookup.COUNT)
+				|| ct.equals(UnitLookup.INDEX) || ct.equals(UnitLookup.IDENTIFIER);
+	}
+
